title: 10 best herbal creams for gastritis

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with gastritis because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The licorice root extract in these creams helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, promoting healing and comfort. Additionally, the natural anti-oxidants present in Glycyrrhiza glabra help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the stomach lining, thereby alleviating symptoms of gastritis such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain, making it a natural and effective remedy for gastritis sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating gastritis symptoms.

Flavonoids: They possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation, thus providing relief from gastritis.

Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and protective effects on the stomach lining, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent damage caused by gastritis, while also promoting healing of the stomach lining.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to protect the stomach lining.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and glycosides that help to soothe stomach irritation and reduce inflammation.
Barks: They are a good source of glycosides and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation, protect the stomach lining, and alleviate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 200 milliliters of vegetable oil for 2 weeks.
Filter the extracted mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Combine 50 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler to melt slowly.
Mix the melted wax and shea butter with 20 milliliters of the extracted glycyrrhiza glabra oil and 10 milliliters of distilled water.
Pour the mixture into small glass containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach lining. The active compound, gingerol, has been shown to inhibit the production of stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's natural antioxidant properties help to protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress, promoting healing and reducing inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of gastritis and promoting overall digestive health. This makes ginger a valuable natural remedy for gastritis sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating gastritis symptoms.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage and promote healing.

Volatile oils (specifically, zingiberene and bisabolol): These oils have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce pain and inflammation in the stomach, as well as prevent further irritation and damage.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help to soothe and calm the stomach, reducing inflammation and pain associated with gastritis.

Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties, helping to ease symptoms of gastritis such as nausea, bloating, and stomach pain.

Barks: Although not as commonly used, the bark of Zingiber officinale contains bioactive compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and promote digestion, making it a potential ingredient in creams for gastritis relief.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of dried zingiber officinale roots in 500ml of coconut oil at 70c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Add 10g of beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture at 60c for 30 minutes.
Blend the mixture thoroughly to ensure the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis, such as stomach pain and acid reflux. Aloe vera creams also promote healing and protect the mucous lining of the stomach, allowing the digestive system to recover and function properly. This natural remedy provides relief from gastritis symptoms and supports overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the stomach, promoting faster recovery from gastritis.
Glycoproteins: Act as a protective barrier to the stomach lining, preventing further irritation and damage from acid and enzymes.
Saponins: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach, reduce swelling, and prevent further inflammation associated with gastritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside Aloe vera leaves is used due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm irritated stomachs and reduce inflammation.
Gel from the Leaves: The clear, jelly-like substance inside Aloe vera leaves is used for its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and promote healing in the stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100 grams of aloe vera gel with 50 grams of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the mixture and melt it in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Stir in 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 10 grams of distilled water into the melted mixture.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and refrigerate it for 30 minutes to set.
Remove the cream from the jar and whip it using a hand mixer for 5 minutes until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with gastritis because of their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as pain and discomfort. It also has a protective effect on the mucous membrane, preventing further irritation and damage. Additionally, curcumin has antimicrobial properties, which help control the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to gastritis. This makes Curcuma longa creams a natural and effective remedy for soothing gastritis and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help with gastritis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, which can alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce inflammation associated with gastritis.
Tumerone: Tumerone, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage caused by gastritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain high concentrations of curcumin, a bioactive compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Roots: Roots are used because they are also a rich source of curcumin, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage.
Seeds: Seeds are used because they contain a compound called demethoxycurcumin, which has been shown to have gastroprotective effects and help heal gastritis-related ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Melt 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 40-50 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of curcuma longa powder with 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the curcuma longa mixture in the double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Blend the melted coconut oil mixture with the curcuma longa mixture in a high-speed blender.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the digestive tract. The active compounds in Foeniculum vulgare, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis, including pain and discomfort. Additionally, Foeniculum vulgare creams may help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, improving digestion and reducing symptoms of gastritis. Its natural and gentle properties make it a popular choice for treating gastritis.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds help with gastritis by reducing inflammation and protecting the gastric mucosa from damage caused by acid and enzymes.
Terpinen-4-ol: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe the stomach lining, reduce inflammation, and alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Anethole: This phenylpropene derivative has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the stomach muscles, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves are used to make creams for gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive system.
Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are used to make creams for gastritis due to their carminative effects that help alleviate digestive discomfort and reduce inflammation.
Buds: Foeniculum vulgare buds are used to make creams for gastritis due to their antimicrobial properties that help protect the digestive system from infections and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds and store them in a cool dry place.
Steep the dried foeniculum vulgare seeds in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture and add 1 tablespoon of beeswax to 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a double boiler at 160 to 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Add 2 tablespoons of the strained foeniculum vulgare liquid mixture to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel of the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis such as heartburn and abdominal pain. Additionally, the cooling and moisturizing effects of Aloe vera can help to protect the mucous membranes in the stomach, promoting healing and reducing discomfort associated with gastritis. This makes Aloe vera a popular natural remedy for soothing gastritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: They help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
Friedelanes and Aliphatic Hydroxy Acids: These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the mucous membrane and reduce acid production in the stomach, alleviating gastritis.

Phenolic Acids (e.g., Caffeic acid, Ferulic acid): They exhibit antioxidant properties, which help prevent oxidative stress and inflammation, thus reducing gastritis symptoms and promoting healing of the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most used part of Aloe barbadensis for making creams for gastritis because they contain high amounts of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Stems: Stems of Aloe barbadensis are used because they contain a higher concentration of aloin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.

Gel (from Leaves): The gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe barbadensis is used to make creams for gastritis because it contains mucilages, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aloe barbadensis gel and mix it with 20 grams of distilled water to create a base.
Melt 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
In a separate bowl, combine 10 grams of glycerin, 5 grams of vitamin e oil, and 5 grams of stearic acid.
Slowly add the melted beeswax mixture to the aloe barbadensis mixture, stirring continuously for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool to room temperature, and whip it with a hand mixer until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense creams helps with gastritis because they contain isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. The herbal extract's ability to calm and protect the digestive tract helps to alleviate symptoms of gastritis, such as pain, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, Trifolium pratense may help to balance the gut microbiome, promoting a healthy digestive environment and reducing the risk of gastritis flare-ups. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These phytoestrogens help alleviate gastritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which can contribute to gastritis.
Saponins: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation associated with gastritis.

Flavonoids: Specifically, kaempferol and quercetin, flavonoids found in red clover, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe gastritis.
Flowers: They are used because they contain isoflavones that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the stomach.
Roots: They are used for their saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and combat infections in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of trifolium pratense flowers at the peak of blooming season for optimal potency.
Combine the harvested flowers with 500ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solids.
Mix 10% of the infused oil with 90% of beeswax to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties and soothing effects on the digestive system. The peppermint oil in these creams can relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis. Additionally, mentha x piperita creams can help to calm digestive spasms and ease nausea, providing relief from the symptoms of gastritis. This natural remedy can help to promote healing and reduce the severity of gastritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis.
Catechol: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating gastritis symptoms.
Royleanone: Displays anti-inflammatory and gastroprotective properties, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage, providing relief from gastritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of menthol and menthone, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe gastric irritation.
Stems: The stems are used because they contain menthol and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from gastritis symptoms.

Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are also used, as they contain menthol and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and 10 grams of flowers from the garden.
Steam distill the extracted plant materials for 3 hours to obtain essential oil.
Combine 50 grams of vegetable wax and 10 grams of cocoa butter in a double boiler.
Add 10 milliliters of mentha x piperita essential oil to the melted wax mixture and stir well.
Pour the final cream mixture into tin containers and let it solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with gastritis because they contain quinine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. The quinine in Cinchona officinalis creams helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms such as abdominal pain and nausea. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce the production of stomach acid, which can contribute to gastritis. This natural approach can provide a gentle and effective way to manage gastritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach, alleviating gastritis symptoms.
Quinovic acid glycosides: These compounds have a protective effect on the stomach lining, preventing irritation and reducing inflammation caused by gastritis.
Quinic acid: Quinic acid has a soothing effect on the stomach, reducing acid secretion and promoting healing of the stomach lining, which helps alleviate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Alkaloids from the leaves of Cinchona officinalis are used in creams to treat gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis contain alkaloids that help to alleviate gastritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive tract.
Leaves: The leaves also contain quinine and other alkaloids, which contribute to the anti-inflammatory effects of creams used to treat gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried cinchona officinalis bark during the summer months when it contains high alkaloid content.
Dry the harvested cinchona officinalis bark in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to preserve properties.
Grind the dried cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 30g of the ground cinchona officinalis powder with 120g of distilled water and 60g of glycerin in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture in the double boiler for 30 minutes at 60°c to create a uniform cream consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis creams helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The marshmallow root, the key ingredient in these creams, contains mucilages that form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation. This helps to calm digestive issues such as gastritis, ulcers, and acid reflux. The cream's soothing action also promotes healing of the stomach lining, allowing it to recover from inflammation and irritation, thereby alleviating gastritis symptoms. Regular use can provide long-term relief.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis.
Althaea flavonoids: Have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the stomach lining, and may also help to protect against stomach acid damage.
Saponins: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, contributing to the relief of gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of mucilage in Althaea officinalis, which helps to soothe and protect the digestive tract.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Stems: The stems also contain mucilage, similar to the roots, which can provide a protective barrier for the digestive tract and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100 grams of dried althaea officinalis root in 500 milliliters of hot water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler until melted.
Combine the infused liquid with 20 milliliters of glycerin and 10 milliliters of distilled water.
Add the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture to the liquid and stir until thickened.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for gastritis are topical treatments containing natural herbs that help soothe and calm the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis. The best herbal creams for gastritis include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Aloe vera, Curcuma longa, Foeniculum vulgare, Aloe barbadensis, Trifolium pratense, Mentha x piperita, Cinchona officinalis, and Althaea officinalis. If left untreated, gastritis can lead to severe abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting, impacting daily life and relationships, making everyday tasks a struggle, and even causing weight loss and fatigue.
